codeReview.summary: The feature substantially implements the backend plugin registry, lifecycle hook dispatch, typed contracts, and comprehensive tests, but it has a blocking type-safety/runtime correctness gap in hook registration and dispatch.

issues.description: _validate_hooks only validates handler callability and never validates that hook keys are HookType values. Non-HookType keys can be accepted and stored, violating the typed hook API contract and enabling invalid runtime state.

dispatch_hook logs failures using hook_type.value, assuming hook_type is a HookType enum. With invalid hook keys admitted via plugin_registry, a failing handler can trigger AttributeError during logging, aborting dispatch and breaking failure isolation behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker reported a procedure deviation for baseline validation even though mission guidance explicitly documents known pre-existing frontend test failures. This indicates procedure/guidance misalignment that causes noisy deviation reporting.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.deviations references step '1.3 Baseline Validation'; mission AGENTS.md lines 57-60 document known pre-existing bun/vitest failures in db/node_provider_plugins.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared test command includes frontend tests that are known to fail in this mission context, creating predictable false negatives for backend-only feature work.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml line 6 defines `test: uv run pytest -x -q --tb=short && bun run test --run`; mission AGENTS.md lines 59-60 note these frontend failures are pre-existing and unrelated.

summary: Fail: core functionality is mostly present, but missing hook-type key validation allows invalid hook registrations that can crash hook dispatch and violate lifecycle failure-isolation guarantees.
